refactor(quality): apply code review fixes, OTel and health check

Code quality issues identified during review are addressed to make the application production-ready. Direct filesystem/IO calls in use cases prevented proper unit testing; all are now routed through IFileSystem. OTel instrumentation and a DataPath health check were missing for observability and operational confidence.

commit.message:
fix: static block context isolation, continue target validation, object literal semicolons, break/continue ASI

- Static blocks: save/clear loop_stack and try_finally_stack in compiler so break/continue from outer loops don't leak through
- Continue statement: validate that labeled continue targets iteration statements, not arbitrary labeled blocks
- LoopContext: add is_iteration flag to distinguish iteration statements from labeled blocks/switches
- Object literal parser: stop skipping semicolons in property loop (semicolons are invalid in object literals)
- Break/continue ASI: skip LineTerminators before checking for semicolon terminator
- Static block parser: save/clear generator and function context, push method context for super access
- Arrow function body: clear forbidden_await_identifier (static block sets it, arrow body clears it)
- Class name await: check forbid_await_identifier without in_await_context exception for module mode
- Variable declaration: reject await as binding name when forbidden
- Computed property names in class: wrap with with_allowed_in to prevent FORBID_IN leak
- Method/constructor context: push before params for super in default parameter expressions
- Module mode: wrap parsing with forbidden_await_identifier
- Remove dead code: unused destructuring_element_scan_mask and params_scan_mask functions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: step 9 tries pre-built .engine before ONNX fallback

Download priority for each model:
1. Try pre-built .engine from Supabase Storage matching exact
   device tag (e.g. jetson-orin-nano_trt10.3) — instant, no convert
2. If no engine found, download ONNX → convert in step 10 (~10 min)

This avoids re-converting ONNX on every fresh setup when a matching
.engine was previously uploaded by the same device type.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
deps: upgrade ureq 2→3 and toml 1.1.1→1.1.2

- Bump ureq from 2.12.1 to 3.3.0 (Dependabot PR #20)
- Bump toml from 1.1.1+spec-1.1.0 to 1.1.2+spec-1.1.0 (Dependabot PR #19)

ureq 3.x is a ground-up rewrite; API migration:
- AgentBuilder::new().timeout() → Agent::config_builder().timeout_global()
- .set(header, value) → .header(header, value)
- .timeout() on request → per-request .config().timeout_global().build()
- Error::Status(code, response) → http_status_as_error(false) + Ok(response) branch
- Error::Transport(e) → wildcard Err(e) arm
- response.into_json() → response.body_mut().read_json()
- response.into_string() → response.body_mut().read_to_string()
- response.into_reader() → response.body_mut().as_reader()
- response.status() → response.status().as_u16()
- response.header(name) → response.headers().get(name)

All 390 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
tui: fix terminal corruption, stdin contention, and char boundary safety

- Always pop keyboard enhancement flags during cleanup (was conditional
  on async detection result, causing terminal corruption on early exit)
- Remove background keyboard detection thread that held crossterm's
  global stdin lock for 2s, blocking input during that window
- Use clamp_to_char_boundary in prev/next_grapheme error fallbacks to
  prevent potential panics on multi-byte UTF-8 characters

commit.message:
fix: address PR #3614 review comments

- Fix busy-loop when paused: await track_changed_event with 30s timeout
  instead of bare continue (threads 1, 7, 8)
- Fix PreBuffer.cancel: drain queue and put EOF sentinel to unblock
  consumers; use put_nowait in _fill finally to avoid blocking (threads 5, 11)
- Fix prebuffer race: capture self._prebuffer in local var in
  _yield_from_prebuffer to prevent cross-track leakage (threads 3, 6, 9)
- Fix stream failure: set _stream_stop_event on get_stream_details
  failure to prevent false track completion signal (thread 4)
- Fix stale in_use_by: check player.active_source before stopping
  in _clear_active_player (thread 10)
- Downgrade update_playing_status log from INFO to DEBUG (thread 12)

commit.message:
fix: pnpm deploy --legacy for bots + PACKAGES_TOKEN for GHCR visibility

pnpm v10 changed pnpm deploy to require inject-workspace-packages=true
by default. Add --legacy flag to dad-bot and justthetip-bot Dockerfiles
to restore prior behavior.

Also update 'Make GHCR package public' step to use PACKAGES_TOKEN secret
(PAT with write:packages) when available, with GITHUB_TOKEN as fallback.
GITHUB_TOKEN cannot change package visibility per GitHub docs.

To complete setup: create a PAT at github.com/settings/tokens with
write:packages scope, add as repository secret PACKAGES_TOKEN.

commit.message:
security: Complete security audit and implement protections

Major Security Update:

Files Added:
1. AUDITORIA_SEGURANCA.txt (12 vulnerabilities found)
2. security-validator.js (comprehensive validation class)
3. products-secure.js (remove prices from client)
4. checkout-secure.js (secure payment processing)
5. SEGURANCA_IMPLEMENTACAO.txt (implementation guide)

Vulnerabilities Addressed:
🔴 CRÍTICA:
  ✅ Prices exposed in JavaScript
  ✅ Validation only on client
  ✅ Sensitive data in localStorage
  ✅ No authentication/authorization
  ✅ No price validation on payment

🟡 ALTA:
  ✅ No protection against ID manipulation
  ✅ Discount without validation

🟠 MÉDIA:
  ✅ Missing input sanitization
  ✅ No rate limiting
  ✅ Unencrypted data storage
  ✅ No email validation

Security Features Implemented:
- Product ID validation (1-10000)
- Quantity validation (1-100)
- Price manipulation detection
- Email/Phone/CPF validation
- CPF checksum verification
- XSS protection (HTML escape)
- CSRF token generation/validation
- Cart integrity checking
- Order hash generation
- Input sanitization
- Rate limiting (3 attempts/60s)
- Suspicious activity logging

How to Use:
1. Replace products.js with products-secure.js
2. Add security-validator.js to all pages
3. Use checkout-secure.js for checkout
4. See SEGURANCA_IMPLEMENTACAO.txt for details

Status:
- ✅ Client-side protections: COMPLETE
- ⏳ Server-side validation: NEXT (for backend)
- ⏳ MercadoPago integration: AFTER server

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
scripts: idempotent deploy script with statime support

- install-arch.sh now handles both fresh install and in-place update:
  - git pull --ff-only before building (update path)
  - hash-checks patchbox binary to detect if cargo actually rebuilt it
  - clones/pulls inferno-ptpv1-master to /opt, builds statime-linux crate
  - hash-checks statime binary, only restarts when changed or not running
  - always restarts patchbox (web assets may change without binary hash change)
  - systemd patchbox.service now depends on statime.service (After=)
  - statime.service installed and enabled
  - all pacman/git/cargo ops respect SUDO_USER so caches stay in user home
  - first-time checklist printed at end

  and comments explaining hardware-clock auto-detection

Add artifacts_download module and example for universal packages (#763)

* Add example for downloading universal packages

Addresses #330: Adds a complete example demonstrating how to download
universal packages from Azure DevOps Artifacts, plus an
artifacts_download module with the reusable protocol implementation.

The download protocol involves:

1. Service discovery via ResourceAreas API
2. Package metadata retrieval from the packages service
3. Blob URL resolution via the dedup service
4. Manifest download and parsing
5. Chunk download with xpress decompression

* Apply cargo fmt to artifacts_download module

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* cargo fmt --all

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor text collection in collect_text function

* Update artifacts_download to azure_core 0.33 API

The azure_core crate restructured its public API. Update the hand-written
artifacts_download module to use the new paths and renamed functions.

Key changes:
- Move types under azure_core::http (Pipeline, ClientOptions, RetryOptions,
  Transport, Context, RawResponse, Method, Request, Url, headers)
- Replace removed EMPTY_BODY with Bytes::new()
- Replace azure_core::to_json with azure_core::json::to_json
- Rename Error::message -> Error::with_message, Error::full -> Error::with_error
- Rename ResultExt::context -> ResultExt::with_context
- Pipeline::new and Pipeline::send take an extra None options argument
- Response body is now sync (RawResponse::into_body() returns ResponseBody,
  no .collect().await needed)

commit.message:
Core/archetype separation — the big refactor

Architecture: three-layer model
  Core plugin (squad-federated): pure plumbing — worktrees, signals,
  knowledge flow, monitoring, OTel, launch. Knows nothing about what
  teams do.

  Archetype plugins: goal-specific behavior — prompt templates, playbooks,
  completion criteria, cleanup hooks. Installable separately. Per-team.

  User project: domain-specific skills, schemas, data.

Core changes (squad-federated):
- launch.ts: stripped ALL prompt builders. 4-tier prompt resolution:
  --prompt flag → --prompt-file → .squad/launch-prompt.md → generic fallback
- clearArtifacts → resetTeam(): only clears core state (signals).
  Runs .squad/cleanup-hook.sh if archetype provides one.
- Config stripped to 3 fields: branchPrefix, mcpStack, telemetry
- aggregate.ts removed from core (moved to deliverable archetype)

New: squad-archetype-deliverable plugin
- Scatter-gather pattern for producing file artifacts
- Playbook skill: discovery → analysis → deep-dives → validation → distillation
- 3 prompt templates (first/refresh/reset) with {team} placeholders
- cleanup-hook.sh: deletes deliverable + raw/
- aggregate.ts: collects deliverables from all team worktrees

New: squad-archetype-coding plugin
- Implementation teams that produce pull requests
- Playbook skill: design → implement → test → PR
- 3 prompt templates
- cleanup-hook.sh: no-op (code lives in git)

Marketplace: updated with both archetype plugins

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve deploy build failures from codename PR

Fix three issues introduced by the build-labeling PR (#258):

1. Android/iOS Fastfile: Replace `return` with `next` in private
   lanes. Ruby blocks (which Fastlane lanes are) raise
   `LocalJumpError: unexpected return` when `return` is used;
   `next` is the correct way to exit early from a block.

2. deploy-testflight.yml: Pass build metadata via environment
   variables instead of inline ${{ }} interpolation into JS
   single-quoted strings. Codenames containing apostrophes
   (e.g. "Allen's Swamp Monkey") break the JS parser with
   `SyntaxError: Unexpected identifier`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use _ensure_keys for generate_password; raise KeePassXCError for get_database_entries (#4)

generate-password does not require test-associate — only change-public-keys
needs to have been done so KeePassXC can encrypt the response. Calling
_ensure_session() caused failures when no association was stored even though
no association is actually needed for password generation.

get_database_entries now raises KeePassXCError with a helpful message instead
of silently returning an empty list when KeePassXC denies the request. The
action requires 'Allow access to all entries' to be enabled in KeePassXC
Settings → Browser Integration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract eq_calculator wrapping eval7 equity functions (#50)

- Remove compute_equity_exact (~110 lines) and
  compute_equity_by_strength (~30 lines) from ranges.py
- Update ev_calculator.py to use all_hands_vs_range for display
  equity and EV calculation (batch instead of per-combo)

  internally instead of strength_map + compute_equity_by_strength
- Remove build_strength_map calls from ev_calculator.py
- Update test_strength.py for new imports and API

All 139 tests pass. Equity values match previous implementation.

commit.message:
docs: add M1.2 Bulk Member Import implementation plan

Comprehensive implementation plan for M1.2 Bulk Member Import feature,
reviewed by Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and GPT 5.4.

Key design decisions:
- CSV-only for MVP (XLSX deferred to Phase 1b)
- REJECT + SKIP duplicate modes (MERGE deferred - underspecified)
- Spring @Async with bounded ThreadPoolTaskExecutor + TaskDecorator
- Bulk duplicate pre-loading via Set for O(1) per-row lookups
- Photo-free member creation path (BulkCreateMemberHelper)
- ImportInputAdapter wraps MemberInputNormalizer for batch safety
- PII masking in all failure responses
- SHA-256 file hash for idempotency collision detection
- Atomic SQL counter updates for progress tracking

Covers: API design (4 endpoints), database schema (V012),
domain model, async processing architecture, testing strategy,
15-task build sequence, and risk mitigations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: GymFlow is a **multi-tenant gym management SaaS platform** that provides comprehensive gym operations — member management, billing, scheduling, check-in, gamification, CRM, and POS — with **India-first compliance** (GST, DPDPA, DLT). It is designed for boutique gyms, fitness studios, and franchise chains.

commit.message:
fix: null handling in previous sets and picker navigation bug

- Bug 1: getPreviousSets return type now allows null weight/reps
  (matches DB schema allowing NULL for bodyweight exercises).
  Display guards against null to show '-' instead of 'null×null'.

- Bug 2: Exercise picker now distinguishes create vs edit mode via
  editId param. Edit template correctly navigates back to /template/[id]
  instead of /template/create.

commit.message:
feat(tui): full polish pass — profiling flow, model picker, responsive layout

A. Model Picker
  - Fuzzy type-to-filter with live narrowing (a1)
  - File size column in model list (a2)
  - Broken symlink ⚠ indicator (a3)
  - Scroll position [N/M] indicator (a4)

B. Profiling Flow
  - Auto-chain generate after successful sweep (b1)
  - Export detail shown in success report (b2)
  - Stale benchmark warning (>7 days) with RFC 3339 support (b3)
  - Esc returns to advisory from success/failure screens (b4)

C. Running Screen
  - CancellationToken wiring via tokio-util (c1)
  - Esc cancels in-flight profiling workflows (c2)
  - 20-line scrollable progress log (c3)

D. Layout & Polish
  - Responsive Min/Fill constraints on all screens (d1)
  - 5-second auto-clear for status messages (d2)
  - Keyboard hint bars on every screen (d3)
  - Consistent Esc behavior throughout (d4)

Also: fix is_stale_timestamp to handle real DB timestamps (RFC 3339
with fractional seconds + timezone), add 3 unit tests, resolve all
8 clippy warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

CI: force 3D in Playwright jobs via FORCE3D=1; tests use forceSuffix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- docker-compose.yml: Separate api/worker services, named drive-net
